titel: The Commission has approved a state aid measure of €402 million from the Spanish government to road transport companies affected by rising fuel prices.
html: European Commission Press Release, Brussels, June 29, 2026. The European Commission has approved a €402 million state aid program from Spain to assist transportation companies affected by rising fuel prices as a result of the crisis in the Middle East.
tldr: Spain is receiving assistance to provide financial support to transportation companies affected by high fuel prices. This initiative aims to help businesses that operate trucks and other vehicles.
lattlast:
The European Commission, the body responsible for governing the EU's common regulations, has approved a subsidy from Spain. The state will provide €402 million to companies in the road transport sector.

The reason for this support is that fuel prices have become significantly more expensive due to the crisis in the Middle East. This makes it difficult for companies that transport goods using trucks.

By receiving these funds, transport companies can better manage the high costs of fuel. The decision was made on June 29, 2026.
